title: Chlamydomonas reinhardtii cell wall extraction with perchlorate
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8fpo8","text":"This protocols describe the steps required for the perchlorate extraction of cell wall proteins from Chlamydomonas reinhardtii. warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ge71","text":"Sodium perchlorate is a oxidizing agent, make sure to read the hazard information. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 2 Molarity (M) Sodium Perchlorate Sodium perchlorate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #410241 (CAS: 7601-89-0) Centrifuge tubes Pipettes and tips Centrifuge
Sodium perchlorate has the following safety concerns. Sodium Perchlorate Prepare the solution in ddH20, by slowly adding the desired amount of Sodium perchlorate to ddH20

Grow cells until the beginning of the stationary phase. ( Until obtained the highest amount of cells ) Count the cells concentration, and harvest the target amount of cells by centrifugation 4000 rcf, 25 °C, 10 minutes . ( 1 x 10^8 cells to 10 x 10^8 cells per mL of 2 Molarity (M) sodium perchlorate were tested) Remove the supernatant decantation or pipetting Wash the cells with fresh TAP media, by ressuspending them in fresh media, followed by centrifugation 4000 rcf, 25 °C, 10 minutes Remove the supernatant decantation or pipetting

Add the desired amount of sodium perchlorate solution. ( ex. From a 1 x 10^7 cells/mL culture, harvest by centrifugation 1.5 mL of culture, and after washing, add 150 uL of sodium perchlorate solution ) Mix by vortexing or pipetting Centrifuge at max speed (ex. For smal volumes 20000 rcf, 25 °C, 1 minute . For larger volumes lower speed can be used for longer time 4000 rcf, 25 °C, 10 minutes ) Recover the supernatant containing the extracted proteins.
creator: Joao Vitor Molino
